source: *> \brief \b DLABAD * * =========== DOCUMENTATION =========== * * Online html documentation available at * http://www.netlib.org/lapack/explore-html/ * *> \htmlonly *> Download DLABAD + dependencies *> *> [TGZ] *> *> [ZIP] *> *> [TXT] *> \endhtmlonly * * Definition: * =========== * * SUBROUTINE DLABAD( SMALL, LARGE ) * * .. Scalar Arguments .. * DOUBLE PRECISION LARGE, SMALL * .. * * *> \par Purpose: * ============= *> *> \verbatim *> *> DLABAD is a no-op and kept for compatibility reasons. It used *> to correct the overflow/underflow behavior of machines that *> are not IEEE-754 compliant. *> *> \endverbatim * * Arguments: * ========== * *> \param[in,out] SMALL *> \verbatim *> SMALL is DOUBLE PRECISION *> On entry, the underflow threshold as computed by DLAMCH. *> On exit, the unchanged value SMALL. *> \endverbatim *> *> \param[in,out] LARGE *> \verbatim *> LARGE is DOUBLE PRECISION *> On entry, the overflow threshold as computed by DLAMCH. *> On exit, the unchanged value LARGE. *> \endverbatim * * Authors: * ======== * *> \author Univ. of Tennessee *> \author Univ. of California Berkeley *> \author Univ. of Colorado Denver *> \author NAG Ltd. * *> \ingroup labad * * ===================================================================== SUBROUTINE DLABAD( SMALL, LARGE ) * * -- LAPACK auxiliary routine -- * -- LAPACK is a software package provided by Univ. of Tennessee, -- * -- Univ. of California Berkeley, Univ. of Colorado Denver and NAG Ltd..-- * * .. Scalar Arguments .. DOUBLE PRECISION LARGE, SMALL * .. * * ===================================================================== * * .. Intrinsic Functions .. INTRINSIC LOG10, SQRT * .. * .. Executable Statements .. * * If it looks like we're on a Cray, take the square root of * SMALL and LARGE to avoid overflow and underflow problems. * * IF( LOG10( LARGE ).GT.2000.D0 ) THEN * SMALL = SQRT( SMALL ) * LARGE = SQRT( LARGE ) * END IF * RETURN * * End of DLABAD * END
